name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: Robert, I beg of you,” Ned pleaded, “hear what you are saying. You are talking of murdering a child.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard恳求Robert听清自己的命令本质：是在谋杀一个child。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard拒绝用princess、heir或threat等政治词汇抽象受害者，先把命令还原为murdering a child。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “The whore is pregnant!” The king’s fist slammed down on the council table loud as a thunderclap. “I warned you this would happen, Ned. Back in the barrowlands, I warned you, but you did not care to hear it. Well, you’ll hear it now. I want them dead, mother and child both, and that fool Viserys as well. Is that plain enough for you? I want them dead.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert因Daenerys怀孕暴怒，要求mother、unborn child和Viserys全部死亡。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert连续重复I want them dead，把恐惧转为绝对命令；把mother与胎儿并列也消除了等待事实发展的空间。
blocks.background_zh: **Daenerys现状：** 十四岁、与Khal Drogo成婚并怀孕；性别尚由情报声称为boy，并非本章直接医学确认。

blocks.original_text: The other councillors were all doing their best to pretend that they were somewhere else. No doubt they were wiser than he was. Eddard Stark had seldom felt quite so alone. “You will dishonor yourself forever if you do this.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 其他councillors假装置身事外，Eddard独自警告此举会永久羞辱Robert。
blocks.literary_note_zh: councillors的回避不是中立，他们随后都会表态；沉默首先表现出没人愿抢在king怒火前承担立场。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Then let it be on my head, so long as it is done. I am not so blind that I cannot see the shadow of the axe when it is hanging over my own neck.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert愿承担污名，只求消除悬在自己颈上的axe。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “There is no axe,” Ned told his king. “Only the shadow of a shadow, twenty years removed . . . if it exists at all.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说所谓axe只是二十年外的影子，甚至可能根本不存在。
blocks.literary_note_zh: shadow of a shadow强调威胁经过血缘、时间、海洋与不确定未来多重折射，Eddard拒绝把可能性当迫近事实。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “If?” Varys asked softly, wringing powdered hands together. “My lord, you wrong me. Would I bring ties to king and council?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys轻声反问Eddard是否怀疑自己会向king和council带来lies。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned looked at the eunuch coldly. “You would bring us the whisperings of a traitor half a world away, my lord. Perhaps Mormont is wrong. Perhaps he is lying.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard指出消息来自半个世界外的traitor Jorah，可能错误或撒谎。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard准确指出情报链含有incentivized source：Jorah有求于crown，因此既可能真报也可能迎合。
blocks.background_zh: **Jorah Mormont：** Daenerys身边exile knight，向Varys传递消息并希望获得royal pardon，因此消息源有利益动机。

blocks.original_text: “Ser Jorah would not dare deceive me,” Varys said with a sly smile. “Rely on it, my lord. The princess is with child.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys说Jorah不敢骗自己，坚持princess怀孕。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “So you say. If you are wrong, we need not fear. If the girl miscarries, we need not fear. If she births a daughter in place of a son, we need not fear. If the babe dies in infancy, we need not fear.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard列出miscarriage、daughter、infant death等多种不构成威胁的可能。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 一串if展示风险树而非单一路径；他的伦理结论不只靠概率，但概率削弱“必须现在杀”的必要性。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “But if it is a boy?” Robert insisted. “If he lives?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert追问若生下存活的boy怎么办。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “The narrow sea would still lie between us. I shall fear the Dothraki the day they teach their horses to run on water.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说narrow sea仍在，等Dothraki学会让马跑水上才值得害怕。
blocks.literary_note_zh: horses run on water用讽刺把Dothraki陆战优势与narrow sea障碍具象化，也可能低估未来运输联盟。
blocks.background_zh: **narrow sea：** Westeros与Essos之间海域；Dothraki强于陆战，尚无本章可见的大规模渡海能力。

blocks.original_text: The king took a swallow of wine and glowered at Ned across the council table. “So you would counsel me to do nothing until the dragonspawn has landed his army on my shores, is that it?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert把等待描述成直到Targaryen army登陆才行动。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “This ‘dragonspawn’ is in his mother’s belly,” Ned said. “Even Aegon did no conquering until after he was weaned.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard强调所谓dragonspawn仍在腹中，连Aegon也是断奶后才征服。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Gods! You are stubborn as an aurochs, Stark.” The king looked around the council table. “Have the rest of you mislaid your tongues? Will no one talk sense to this frozen-faced fool?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert骂Eddard像aurochs般固执，并逼其他councillors表态。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert要求杀死pregnant Daenerys、胎儿与Viserys；Eddard从情报不确定、海洋距离和道德污名三方面反对。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Varys gave the king an unctuous smile and laid a soft hand on Ned’s sleeve. “I understand your qualms, Lord Eddard, truly I do. It gave me no joy to bring this grievous news to council. It is a terrible thing we contemplate, a vile thing. Yet we who presume to rule must do vile things for the good of the realm, howevermuch it pains us.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys先承认谋杀vile，却以统治者为realm利益必须做恶来支持行动。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys先承认vile再诉realm，是dirty hands论证：统治责任被用来允许私人道德禁止之事。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Lord Renly shrugged. “The matter seems simple enough to me. We ought to have had Viserys and his sister killed years ago, but His Grace my brother made the mistake of listening to Jon Arryn.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Renly认为多年前就该杀Viserys与Daenerys，Jon的mercy是错误。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Mercy is never a mistake, Lord Renly,” Ned replied. “On the Trident, Ser Barristan here cut down a dozen good men, Robert’s friends and mine. When they brought him to us, grievously wounded and near death, Roose Bolton urged us to cut his throat, but your brother said, ‘I will not kill a man for loyalty, nor for fighting well,’ and sent his own maester to tend Ser Barristan’s wounds.” He gave the king a long cool look. “Would that man were here today.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard以Robert救治敌将Barristan的旧事论证mercy从非错误，并暗问昔日高尚Robert去了哪里。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Barristan旧事把Robert自身最好行为变成反对当前Robert的证据；Would that man were here既怀念也指控。
blocks.background_zh: **Trident旧事：** Barristan为Aerys一方重伤后，Robert拒绝按Roose建议杀死他，反而派maester救治。

blocks.original_text: Robert had shame enough to blush. “It was not the same,” he complained. “Ser Barristan was a knight of the Kingsguard.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert羞愧脸红，却辩称Barristan身份不同。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Whereas Daenerys is a fourteen-year-old girl.” Ned knew he was pushing this well past the point of wisdom, yet he could not keep silent. “Robert, I ask you, what did we rise against Aerys Targaryen for, if not to put an end to the murder of children?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard反驳Daenerys只是十四岁girl，并问Rebellion若非为终止杀害children还有何意义。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard把Rebellion正当性系于终止child murder；若胜者复制Aerys暴行，战争的道德叙事便坍塌。
blocks.background_zh: **Rebellion道德记忆：** Aerys阵营曾涉及对Stark及children的暴行；Eddard以此衡量胜利者当前行为。

blocks.original_text: “To put an end to Targaryens!” the king growled.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert答目的就是终结Targaryens。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Your Grace, I never knew you to fear Rhaegar.” Ned fought to keep the scorn out of his voice, and failed. “Have the years so unmanned you that you tremble at the shadow of an unborn child?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard以Robert从不怕Rhaegar反讽他如今竟怕unborn child。
blocks.literary_note_zh: unmanned与tremble直接攻击Robert男子气概，是危险但针对其核心自我形象的最后劝谏。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Robert purpled. “No more, Ned,” he warned, pointing. “Not another word. Have you forgotten who is king here?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert气紫脸，警告Eddard别忘谁是king。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “No, Your Grace,” Ned replied. “Have you?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard回问Robert自己是否忘了king应成为什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Have you?把“谁拥有权位”翻转成“是否履行king责任”；短句使政治不服从公开化。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Enough!” the king bellowed. “I am sick of talk. I’ll be done with this, or be damned. What say you all?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert结束争论，要求每人直接投票。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “She must be killed,” Lord Renly declared.
blocks.explanation_zh: Renly明确赞成杀死Daenerys。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “We have no choice,” murmured Varys. “Sadly, sadly . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys以悲伤姿态说别无选择。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ser Barristan Selmy raised his pale blue eyes from the table and said, “Your Grace, there is honor in facing an enemy on the battlefield, but none in killing him in his mother’s womb. Forgive me, but I must stand with Lord Eddard.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Barristan认为战场杀敌有honor，杀死母腹中敌人没有，因此支持Eddard。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Barristan把enemy保留为enemy，却要求战场面对，说明反刺杀不等于否认未来威胁。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Grand Maester Pycelle cleared his throat, a process that seemed to take some minutes. “My order serves the realm, not the ruler. Once I counseled King Aerys as loyally as I counsel King Robert now, so I bear this girl child of his no ill will. Yet I ask you this— should war come again, how many soldiers will die? How many towns will burn? How many children will be ripped from their mothers to perish on the end of a spear?” He stroked his luxuriant white beard, infinitely sad, infinitely weary. “Is it not wiser, even kinder, that Daenerys Targaryen should die now so that tens of thousands might live?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle以未来战争的士兵、城镇和children伤亡论证杀一人可救数万人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle使用consequentialist calculus：确定杀一名无辜者换取避免假设性大规模伤亡；预测可靠性决定论证强弱。
blocks.background_zh: **council分歧：** Renly、Varys、Pycelle、Littlefinger支持行动；Eddard与Barristan反对。Robert拥有最终命令权。

blocks.original_text: “Kinder,” Varys said. “Oh, well and truly spoken, Grand Maester. It is so true. Should the gods in their caprice grant Daenerys Targaryen a son, the realm must bleed.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys赞同所谓kinder选择，并把Daenerys son与realm流血直接相连。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Littlefinger was the last. As Ned looked to him, Lord Petyr stifled a yawn. “When you find yourself in bed with an ugly woman, the best thing to do is close your eyes and get on with it,” he declared. “Waiting won’t make the maid any prettier. Kiss her and be done with it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger用与ugly woman同床的粗俗比喻主张尽快完成不愉快任务。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger把国家谋杀类比成不得不完成的性行为，以轻佻语言降低道德重量，并回避谁承担后果。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Kiss her?” Ser Barristan repeated, aghast.
blocks.explanation_zh: Barristan惊问kiss何意。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “A steel kiss,” said Littlefinger.
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger答是steel kiss。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Robert turned to face his Hand. “Well, there it is, Ned. You and Selmy stand alone on this matter. The only question that remains is, who can we find to kill her?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert宣布Eddard与Barristan少数败北，转问由谁执行刺杀。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys、Renly、Pycelle与Littlefinger以realm安全支持刺杀，Barristan独自站在Eddard一边；投票后Robert转向执行。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Mormont craves a royal pardon,” Lord Renly reminded them.
blocks.explanation_zh: Renly提醒Jorah渴望royal pardon。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Desperately,” Varys said, “yet he craves life even more. By now, the princess nears Vaes Dothrak, where it is death to draw a blade. If I told you what the Dothraki would do to the poor man who used one on a khaleesi, none of you would sleep tonight.” He stroked a powdered cheek. “Now, poison . . . the tears of Lys, let us say. Khal Drogo need never know it was not a natural death.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys说Vaes Dothrak拔刀会死，建议用tears of Lys伪装自然死亡。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Varys刚在上一章称tears of Lys杀死Jon，此处立刻把同一毒药作为政策工具，Pycelle的睁眼反应因此值得注意但含义未明。
blocks.background_zh: **Vaes Dothrak／tears of Lys：** sacred city按Dothraki custom不得拔blade流血；Varys因此建议据称clear、sweet且不留痕迹的毒药，也曾称Jon死于此毒。本段Pycelle明显警觉。

blocks.original_text: Grand Maester Pycelle’s sleepy eyes flicked open. He squinted suspiciously at the eunuch.
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle骤然睁眼，怀疑地看向Varys。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Poison is a coward’s weapon,” the king complained.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert嫌poison是coward武器。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned had heard enough. “You send hired knives to kill a fourteen-year-old girl and still quibble about honor?” He pushed back his chair and stood. “Do it yourself, Robert. The man who passes the sentence should swing the sword. Look her in the eyes before you kill her. See her tears, hear her last words. You owe her that much at least.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard指责Robert雇刀杀十四岁girl却还计较honor，要求他按Stark原则亲自看着受刑者执行。
blocks.literary_note_zh: The man who passes the sentence should swing the sword把Winterfell justice原则带入远程刺杀争议：责任不能被distance和hired knife稀释。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Gods,” the king swore, the word exploding out of him as if he could barely contain his fury. “You mean it, damn you.” He reached for the flagon of wine at his elbow, found it empty, and flung it away to shatter against the wall. “I am out of wine and out of patience. Enough of this. Just have it done.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert发现Eddard真心拒绝后砸空flagon，命令无论如何执行。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “I will not be part of murder, Robert. Do as you will, but do not ask me to fix my seal to it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard拒绝在murder命令上加Hand seal。
blocks.literary_note_zh: seal把道德异议变成行政拒绝；没有Hand认证，Eddard不允许自己的office替命令提供合法外观。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: For a moment Robert did not seem to understand what Ned was saying. Defiance was not a dish he tasted often. Slowly his face changed as comprehension came. His eyes narrowed and a flush crept up his neck past the velvet collar. He pointed an angry finger at Ned. “You are the King’s Hand, Lord Stark. You will do as I command you, or I’ll find me a Hand who will.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert起初无法理解defiance，随后以Hand必须服从否则换人威胁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “I wish him every success.” Ned unfastened the heavy clasp that clutched at the folds of his cloak, the ornate silver hand that was his badge of office. He laid it on the table in front of the king, saddened by the memory of the man who had pinned it on him, the friend he had loved. “I thought you a better man than this, Robert. I thought we had made a nobler king.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard摘下silver hand badge辞职，祝下一任成功，并说自己曾以为Robert更高尚。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 摘badge是可见的身份解除，也把Jon亲手授予的office还回导致Jon调查死亡的制度。
blocks.background_zh: **Hand badge：** ornate silver hand象征office，由Robert任命、Jon曾亲自为Eddard佩上；辞还即拒绝继续任职。

blocks.original_text: Robert’s face was purple. “Out,” he croaked, choking on his rage. “Out, damn you, I’m done with you. What are you waiting for? Go, run back to Winterfell. And make certain I never look on your face again, or I swear, I’ll have your head on a spike!”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert命他滚回Winterfell，威胁若再相见就把头插上spike。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned bowed, and turned on his heel without another word. He could feel Robert’s eyes on his back. As he strode from the council chambers, the discussion resumed with scarcely a pause. “On Braavos there is a society called the Faceless Men,” Grand Maester Pycelle offered.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard鞠躬离开；门未关前已听见Pycelle提议Braavos的Faceless Men。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 讨论scarcely a pause显示Eddard辞职并未阻止policy machinery；个人道德退出与实际救下Daenerys并非同一成果。
blocks.background_zh: **Faceless Men：** Braavos的神秘刺客组织，以高昂价格闻名；本章只讨论雇佣可能。

blocks.original_text: “Do you have any idea how costly they are?” Littlefinger complained. “You could hire an army of common sellswords for half the price, and that’s for a merchant. I don’t dare think what they might ask for a princess.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger抱怨Faceless Men刺杀princess费用高得胜过雇一支sellsword army。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger先把murder转成price comparison，财政专业在此服务于刺杀方案优化。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: The closing of the door behind him silenced the voices. Ser Boros Blount was stationed outside the chamber, wearing the long white cloak and armor of the Kingsguard. He gave Ned a quick, curious glance from the corner of his eye, but asked no questions.
blocks.explanation_zh: 门关闭隔绝声音，守门Boros只好奇旁观。
blocks.literary_note_zh: council讨论Jorah、poison与Faceless Men；Eddard拒绝盖seal、摘下Hand badge离场，但刺杀规划没有停。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: The day felt heavy and oppressive as he crossed the bailey back to the Tower of the Hand. He could feel the threat of rain in the air. Ned would have welcomed it. It might have made him feel a trifle less unclean. When he reached his solar, he summoned Vayon Poole. The steward came at once. “You sent for me, my lord Hand?”
blocks.explanation_zh: 沉闷天气让Eddard想以雨洗净参与会议的污秽感；回Tower后他召Vayon。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard秘密安排daughters先行并找船返北，同时想经Dragonstone问Stannis，在归家渴望与未完调查间摇摆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Hand no longer,” Ned told him. “The king and I have quarreled. We shall be returning to Winterfell.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说明自己已不是Hand，准备全员返回Winterfell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard秘密安排daughters先行并找船返北，同时想经Dragonstone问Stannis，在归家渴望与未完调查间摇摆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “I shall begin making arrangements at once, my lord. We will need a fortnight to ready everything for the journey.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Vayon估计整备需要两周。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard秘密安排daughters先行并找船返北，同时想经Dragonstone问Stannis，在归家渴望与未完调查间摇摆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “We may not have a fortnight. We may not have a day. The king mentioned something about seeing my head on a spike.” Ned frowned. He did not truly believe the king would harm him, not Robert. He was angry now, but once Ned was safely out of sight, his rage would cool as it always did.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说可能连一天都没有，却仍相信Robert冷静后不会真伤害旧友。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Robert rage always cool为安全模型；下一段用Rhaegar反例检验“always”并发现并不成立。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Always? Suddenly, uncomfortably, he found himself recalling Rhaegar Targaryen. Fifteen years dead, yet Robert hates him as much as ever. It was a disturbing notion . . . and there was the other matter, the business with Catelyn and the dwarf that Yoren had warned him of last night. That would come to light soon, as sure as sunrise, and with the king in such a black fury . . . Robert might not care a fig for Tyrion Lannister, but it would touch on his pride, and there was no telling what the queen might do.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他忽然想到Robert对死去十五年的Rhaegar仍不消怒，加上Catelyn拘捕Tyrion即将曝光，安全判断动摇。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn/Tyrion消息把私人争吵连接到Stark–Lannister冲突；Eddard开始从离职规划转向撤离规划。
blocks.background_zh: **迫近消息：** Yoren已告诉Eddard Catelyn拘捕Tyrion，且消息正在向Casterly Rock传播。

blocks.original_text: “It might be safest if I went on ahead,” he told Poole. “I will take my daughters and a few guardsmen. The rest of you can follow when you are ready. Inform Jory, but tell no one else, and do nothing until the girls and I have gone. The castle is full of eyes and ears, and I would rather my plans were not known.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard决定带daughters与少量guards先走，要求Jory知情但在他们离开前保持秘密。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard秘密安排daughters先行并找船返北，同时想经Dragonstone问Stannis，在归家渴望与未完调查间摇摆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “As you command, my lord.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Vayon领命。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard秘密安排daughters先行并找船返北，同时想经Dragonstone问Stannis，在归家渴望与未完调查间摇摆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: When he had gone, Eddard Stark went to the window and sat brooding. Robert had left him no choice that he could see. He ought to thank him. It would be good to return to Winterfell. He ought never have left. His sons were waiting there. Perhaps he and Catelyn would make a new son together when he returned, they were not so old yet. And of late he had often found himself dreaming of snow, of the deep quiet of the wolfswood at night.
blocks.explanation_zh: 独处时Eddard既向往sons、Catelyn、新child、snow和wolfswood，又把离开解释成Robert替他做了选择。
blocks.literary_note_zh: snow、sons与new child构成归家诱惑，但ought thank him显示他也在把被迫退场改写成主动释放。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: And yet, the thought of leaving angered him as well. So much was still undone. Robert and his council of cravens and flatterers would beggar the realm if left unchecked . . . or, worse, sell it to the Lannisters in payment of their loans. And the truth of Jon Arryn’s death still eluded him. Oh, he had found a few pieces, enough to convince him that Jon had indeed been murdered, but that was no more than the spoor of an animal on the forest floor. He had not sighted the beast itself yet, though he sensed it was there, lurking, hidden, treacherous.
blocks.explanation_zh: 但未完成的财政、Lannister影响与Jon murder调查又令他愤怒；现有线索只是兽迹，尚未看见野兽本身。
blocks.literary_note_zh: spoor/beast把调查比作tracking：线索能证明猎物可能存在，却还不能识别、定位和捕获。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: It struck him suddenly that he might return to Winterfell by sea. Ned was no sailor, and ordinarily would have preferred the kingsroad, but if he took ship he could stop at Dragonstone and speak with Stannis Baratheon. Pycelle had sent a raven off across the water, with a polite letter from Ned requesting Lord Stannis to return to his seat on the small council. As yet, there had been no reply, but the silence only deepened his suspicions. Lord Stannis shared the secret Jon Arryn had died for, he was certain of it. The truth he sought might very well be waiting for him on the ancient island fortress of House Targaryen.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他想到乘船北返可途经Dragonstone询问Stannis；Stannis沉默使他更确信对方掌握Jon秘密。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 经Dragonstone返家把retreat与最后调查合并，是Eddard试图在安全和unfinished duty间找到路线。
blocks.background_zh: **Dragonstone：** Stannis所在的古Targaryen island fortress；他曾与Jon共同调查且未回应返council请求。

blocks.original_text: And when you have it, what then? Some secrets are safer kept hidden. Some secrets are too dangerous to share, even with those you love and trust. Ned slid the dagger that Catelyn had brought him out of the sheath on his belt. The Imp’s knife. Why would the dwarf want Bran dead? To silence him, surely. Another secret, or only a different strand of the same web?
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard反问得到truth后怎么办，并把dagger、Bran与Jon秘密想成同一web的不同strand。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他已经把Littlefinger所称dagger当the Imp’s knife，说明争议归属在内心语言中仍易固化成事实。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Could Robert be part of it? He would not have thought so, but once he would not have thought Robert could command the murder of women and children either. Catelyn had tried to warn him. You knew the man, she had said. The king is a stranger to you. The sooner he was quit of King’s Landing, the better. If there was a ship sailing north on the morrow, it would be well to be on it.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert杀Daenerys的决定使Eddard首次怀疑king是否也可能涉入其他阴谋，并想起Catelyn说king已成陌生人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert当前明确命令杀child，使Eddard重新评估过去“不可能”判断；这是更新模型，不是发现Robert涉案证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: He summoned Vayon Poole again and sent him to the docks to make inquiries, quietly but quickly. “Find me a fast ship with a skilled captain,” he told the steward. “I care nothing for the size of its cabins or the quality of its appointments, so long as it is swift and safe. I wish to leave at once.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 他再次召Vayon，命其秘密寻找只求快速安全、不求舒适的北行船。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard秘密安排daughters先行并找船返北，同时想经Dragonstone问Stannis，在归家渴望与未完调查间摇摆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Poole had no sooner taken his leave than Tomard announced a visitor. “Lord Baelish to see you, m’lord.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Vayon刚走，Tomard便报告Littlefinger来访。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned was half-tempted to turn him away, but thought better of it. He was not free yet; until he was, he must play their games. “Show him in, Tom.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard本想拒见，却认为自己尚未离开，仍须play their games。
blocks.literary_note_zh: play their games表明辞职没有立即使他退出court信息环境；身份过渡期仍有被观察与利用的风险。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Lord Petyr sauntered into the solar as if nothing had gone amiss that morning. He wore a slashed velvet doublet in cream-and-silver, a grey silk cloak trimmed with black fox, and his customary mocking smile.
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger穿华服、带惯常mocking smile，仿佛早晨无事发生般进门。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned greeted him coldly. “Might I ask the reason for this visit, Lord Baelish?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard冷问来意。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “I won’t detain you long, I’m on my way to dine with Lady Tanda. Lamprey pie and roast suckling pig. She has some thought to wed me to her younger daughter, so her table is always astonishing. If truth be told, I’d sooner marry the pig, but don’t tell her. I do love lamprey pie.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger以Lady Tanda想嫁女和丰盛晚餐开玩笑，说自己宁娶suckling pig但爱lamprey pie。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Don’t let me keep you from your eels, my lord,” Ned said with icy disdain. “At the moment, I cannot think of anyone whose company I desire less than yours.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard叫他去吃eels，并说此刻最不想见的就是他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, I’m certain if you put your mind to it, you could come up with a few names. Varys, say. Cersei. Or Robert. His Grace is most wroth with you. He went on about you at some length after you took your leave of us this morning. The words insolence and ingratitude came into it frequently, I seem to recall.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger列Varys、Cersei、Robert为更差对象，又转述Robert指责Eddard insolence与ingratitude。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned did not honor that with a reply. Nor did he offer his guest a seat, but Littlefinger took one anyway. “After you stormed out, it was left to me to convince them not to hire the Faceless Men,” he continued blithely. “Instead Varys will quietly let it be known that we’ll make a lord of whoever does in the Targaryen girl.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard沉默且不让座，Littlefinger自行坐下，声称自己阻止雇Faceless Men，改由Varys悬赏lordship。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger声称把高成功率方案换成低成功率方案；结果可能确实降低即时风险，但动机与后续仍由他自述。
blocks.background_zh: **执行方案：** council最终不雇Faceless Men，而由Varys散布“杀Daenerys者获lordship”的悬赏。

blocks.original_text: Ned was disgusted. “So now we grant titles to assassins.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard厌恶用title奖励assassin。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Littlefinger shrugged. “Titles are cheap. The Faceless Men are expensive. If truth be told, I did the Targaryen girl more good than you with all your talk of honor. Let some sellsword drunk on visions of lordship try to kill her. Likely he’ll make a botch of it, and afterward the Dothraki will be on their guard. If we’d sent a Faceless Man after her, she’d be as good as buried.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger以titles cheap、Faceless Men expensive解释，并称低水平sellsword更可能失败，反而给Daenerys预警。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他以expected outcome为自己辩护：formal vote支持谋杀，implementation design却让失败更可能；道德与策略被刻意分离。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: Ned frowned. “You sit in council and talk of ugly women and steel kisses, and now you expect me to believe that you tried to protect the girl? How big a fool do you take me for?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard质疑他council中赞同steel kiss、现在却自称保护girl的可信度。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard抓住public words与private claim矛盾，拒绝因聪明效果自动相信善意动机。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Well, quite an enormous one, actually,” said Littlefinger, laughing.
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger笑说自己确实把Eddard当巨大fool。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “Do you always find murder so amusing, Lord Baelish?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问他是否总觉得murder好笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “It’s not murder I find amusing, Lord Stark, it’s you. You rule like a man dancing on rotten ice. I daresay you will make a noble splash. I believe I heard the first crack this morning.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger说好笑的是Eddard像在rotten ice上治理，早晨已听见第一道裂纹。
blocks.literary_note_zh: rotten ice比喻认为court规则表面可站、内部已腐坏；noble splash同时赞美Eddard高尚与预言其失败。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “The first and last,” said Ned. “I’ve had my fill.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说那也是最后一道，自己已经受够。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “When do you mean to return to Winterfell, my lord?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger询问何时回Winterfell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “As soon as I can. What concern is that of yours?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard答越快越好，并反问与他何干。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Littlefinger来访，声称用低成功率悬赏替代Faceless Men间接保护Daenerys，继而以brothel线索诱使Eddard暂缓离开。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在预防性暴力的证据门槛、council责任、Eddard道德原则与撤离风险。

blocks.original_text: “None . . . but if perchance you’re still here come evenfall, I’d be pleased to take you to this brothel your man Jory has been searching for so ineffectually.” Littlefinger smiled. “And I won’t even tell the Lady Catelyn.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger说若Eddard黄昏仍在，就带他去Jory一直没找到的brothel，并拿Catelyn开玩笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: brothel邀请在Eddard即将离城时出现，正对应Jory调查Jon与Stannis曾访问的地点，迫使他在撤退前再做一次选择。
blocks.background_zh: **brothel线索：** former stablehand称Jon与Stannis曾同去一家brothel；Jory一直未找到具体地点。
